The landscape is very similar to Finland, as both share the Fennoscandian shield and boreal forest biomes. However, the extreme flatness seen here is slightly more typical of the Finnish lake district or eastern plains than the more varied Swedish topography.

The taiga landscape of Northwest Russia (Karelia) is geographically nearly identical to Finland and Sweden. The terrain and vegetation are fully compatible, though the road maintenance style is slightly more reminiscent of Nordic countries.

While Sweden and Russia share similar vegetation, the specific combination of extreme flatness and the style of snow-packed rural road maintenance strongly supports Finland over the more mountainous Norway or the different infrastructure patterns often found in Russia.
